gmem1_m_axi_U gmem2_m_axi_U

field.name: Enable
field.description: Master enable for the device interrupt output to the system interrupt controller: 0 = Disabled, 1 = Enabled

field.name: CHAN0_INT_ST
field.description: Channel 0 (ap_done) Interrupt Status. 0 = No Channel 0 interrupt, 1 = Channel 0 interrupt.

field.name: CHAN1_INT_ST
field.description: Channel 1 (ap_ready) Interrupt Status. 0 = No Channel 1 interrupt, 1 = Channel 1 interrupt.

table.keys: Interface, Data Width (SW->HW), Address Width, Latency, Offset, Register, Max Widen Bitwidth, Max Read Burst Length, Max Write Burst Length, Num Read Outstanding, Num Write Outstanding, Resource Estimate
Interface, Data Width, Address Width, Offset, Register
Interface, Register, Offset, Width, Access, Description, Bit Fields
table.column: GIER, 0x04, 32, RW, Global Interrupt Enable Register, 0=Enable
IP_IER, 0x08, 32, RW, IP Interrupt Enable Register, 0=CHAN0_INT_EN 1=CHAN1_INT_EN
IP_ISR, 0x0c, 32, RW, IP Interrupt Status Register, 0=CHAN0_INT_ST 1=CHAN1_INT_ST
a_1, 0x10, 32, W, Data signal of a,
a_2, 0x14, 32, W, Data signal of a,
b_1, 0x1c, 32, W, Data signal of b,
b_2, 0x20, 32, W, Data signal of b,
c_1, 0x28, 32, W, Data signal of c,
c_2, 0x2c, 32, W, Data signal of c,
a_row, 0x34, 32, W, Data signal of a_row,
a_col, 0x3c, 32, W, Data signal of a_col,
b_col, 0x44, 32, W, Data signal of b_col,

table.keys: Argument, HW Interface, HW Type, HW Usage, HW Info
table.column: s_axi_control, register, offset, name=a_1 offset=0x10 range=32
s_axi_control, register, offset, name=a_2 offset=0x14 range=32
s_axi_control, register, offset, name=b_1 offset=0x1c range=32
s_axi_control, register, offset, name=b_2 offset=0x20 range=32
s_axi_control, register, offset, name=c_1 offset=0x28 range=32
s_axi_control, register, offset, name=c_2 offset=0x2c range=32

section.name: M_AXI Burst Information
section.HEADER_NOTE: Note: All burst requests might be further partitioned into multiple requests during RTL generation based on max_read_burst_length or max_write_burst_length settings.

table.keys: HW Interface, Direction, Length, Width, Loop, Loop Location
HW Interface, Variable, Access Location, Direction, Burst Status, Length, Loop, Loop Location
table.column: a, mmult.cpp:181:22, read, Inferred, variable, readA, mmult.cpp:175:5
b, mmult.cpp:192:22, read, Inferred, variable, readB, mmult.cpp:186:5
c, mmult.cpp:218:16, write, Inferred, variable, writeC, mmult.cpp:212:5

Pragma.options: m_axi port = a offset = slave bundle = gmem0
m_axi port = b offset = slave bundle = gmem1
m_axi port = c offset = slave bundle = gmem2

Msg.msg_body: Unsuported scalar variable on pragma 'Resource/Bind_Storage', ignore it.
Since the only kind of statements allowed in a canonical dataflow region are variable declarations and function calls, the compiler may not be able to correctly handle the region
There are a total of 5 such instances of non-canonical statements in the dataflow region

Pragma.options: min = c_size* c_size max = c_size * c_size
min = c_size* c_size max = c_size * c_size
min = c_size* c_size max = c_size * c_size
